text: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. Named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower. Originally constructed as the centerpiece of the 1889 World's Fair, it was initially criticized by some of France's leading artists and intellectuals but has become a global cultural icon of France and one of the most recognizable structures in the world.
